title: PagerDuty Escalation Policy Depth Exhausted Without Acknowledgement
spl:
index=alerts sourcetype="pagerduty:incident"
| where escalation_count >= 4 AND status != "resolved"
| eval age_m=(now()-created_at_epoch)/60
| where age_m > 30
| table incident_number, service_name, escalation_policy, age_m, status
| sort -age_m
description: Deep escalations burn out multiple responder tiers for one root cause.
value: Surfaces incidents paging past normal L1/L2 paths.
implementation: Map policy names to business services; page leadership only after validation.
knownFalsePositives: Deep escalations from real sev-1s, on-call load, or people stepping away at the wrong moment; separate a bad week from a broken policy.
grandmaExplanation: We look for the chain that should page people until someone answers, but runs all the way out, so a real sev-1 is not a silent one.
detailedImplementation:
Prerequisites
• Install and configure: PagerDuty REST API incremental pull.
• Data: `pagerduty:incident` with `escalation_count`, `created_at_epoch`.
• For directory layout and inputs, see docs/implementation-guide.md.

Step 1 — Configure data collection
Map policy names to business services; page leadership only after validation.

Step 2 — Create the search and alert
Run the following search (then save as report or alert; tune time range and threshold):

```spl
index=alerts sourcetype="pagerduty:incident"
| where escalation_count >= 4 AND status != "resolved"
| eval age_m=(now()-created_at_epoch)/60
| where age_m > 30
| table incident_number, service_name, escalation_policy, age_m, status
| sort -age_m
```

Understanding this search
Deep escalations burn out multiple responder tiers for one root cause.

Step 3 — Validate
Compare with the PagerDuty incident timeline and service view for the same window. Reconcile a few incident numbers, state changes, and acknowledgement times. If anything disagrees, check export lag, time zone, and which API fields we map.

Step 4 — Operationalize
Add to a dashboard, wire alert actions, and document ownership. Suggested view: Timeline, Table (open incidents).
